records.ety: [L. desultorius, fr. desultor a leaper, fr. desilire, desultum, to leap down; de + salire to leap. See Saltation.]

quotes: I shot at it [a bird], but it was so desultory that I missed my aim.

defs: Jumping, or passing, from one thing or subject to another, without order or rational connection; without logical sequence; disconnected; immethodical; aimless; as, desultory minds.
quotes: He [Goldsmith] knew nothing accurately; his reading had been desultory.

defs: Out of course; by the way; as a digression; not connected with the subject; as, a desultory remark.
